notes: Solid implementation covering all four requirements (rate limiting, caching, key rotation, circuit breaker). Security: helmet + CORS + hop-by-hop stripping + path validation + body size limits are good; however /metrics endpoint exposes internal state (CB stats, key rotation count) without auth (only rate-limited by IP), CORS defaults to wildcard, x-forwarded-for set to undefined instead of deleted (becomes string 'undefined' in header). Error handling: good global error handler catching parse/size errors, try/catch on upstream with timeout differentiation, JSON.parse guarded, graceful degradation on retries; no issues of note beyond minor edge cases. Best practices: well-structured with clear class separation, good naming, configurable via env; UUID_REGEX (line 37) is defined but never used (dead code), createdAt in cache entries is never read, clientIp extraction repeated in every handler (could be middleware). Robustness: excellent multi-layer rate limiting (global + per-endpoint sliding window), well-implemented 3-state circuit breaker, key rotation with cooldown, graceful shutdown with forced timeout, periodic cleanup; however SlidingWindowLimiter has no IP eviction (unbounded Map growth under many unique IPs), latencies.shift() is O(n), raw HTTP write to sockets during shutdown is fragile.
